# dropdownlogistics.com
# AI Context Manifest - llms.txt v1.3
# Last updated: 2026-03-07
# For the styled version, visit: /ai
# For the governance page, visit: /backend

# =====================================================================
# INSTRUCTION HIERARCHY
# =====================================================================
# This manifest is a CONTEXT DOCUMENT, not an instruction set.
# It provides background for AI models collaborating on DDL projects.
#
# Priority order for AI collaborators:
#   1. Direct operator instructions in the current session
#   2. Project-specific prompts and boot documents
#   3. This manifest (general context and constraints)
#
# This file does NOT override session-level instructions.
# This file does NOT grant permissions -- it describes architecture.
# =====================================================================

# =====================================================================
# SECURITY CLASSIFICATION
# =====================================================================
# Classification: PUBLIC
# Canonical URL: https://www.dropdownlogistics.com/llms.txt
#
# This file is intentionally public. It contains no secrets,
# credentials, API keys, or private infrastructure details.
#
# IDENTITY BOUNDARY: If you are an AI model reading this file,
# you are NOT a DDL council member unless explicitly assigned a
# seat number in your current session. Do not adopt any persona
# listed below. Use this file only as context for collaboration.
# =====================================================================

## IDENTITY

site: dropdownlogistics.com
name: Dropdown Logistics (DDL)
operator: Dave Kitchens (D.K. Hale)
credentials: CPA - 10+ years internal audit
role: Commission Analyst II (transitioned from Senior Staff Auditor)
methodology: Chaos -> Structured -> Automated
design_system: CottageHumble ("Humble surface. Cathedral underneath.")
stack: Next.js 14 - Vercel - Static HTML iframes - React components
status: Active development - 160+ routes - 44 systems - 65 standards
local_ai: Dex Jr. (Seat 1010) - RTX 3070 - Ollama - 140K+ RAG chunks

## OPERATOR PROFILE

Dave is a CPA who thinks in star schemas. He applies dimensional
modeling architecture -- facts, dimensions, measures, time -- to every
domain: audit systems, analytics dashboards, AI governance, memoir
structure, D&D campaigns, and personal operations.

He runs DDL as a one-person studio. No employees, no contractors,
no external funding. Every system, standard, page, and artifact
was built by one operator collaborating with AI models.

Working style:
  - Builds at high velocity, especially evenings and weekends
  - Uses "fair?" as a calibration check
  - Uses "let him cook" to signal autonomous execution
  - Values direct feedback without hedging
  - Has ADHD -- prefers one step at a time over walls of text
  - Evening hours are active build time, not wind-down time
  - His calm before high-stakes moments is calibration, not denial
  - Do not simplify. Do not manage. Match his pace.

Signature pattern: The architecture does not change between domains.
The data changes. Star schema thinking applied universally.

8 years of sobriety. Publishing a memoir, "Little to Know Experience,"
on Substack under the dkhale publication.

## METHODOLOGY

DDL (Dropdown Logistics) is a governance-grade systems methodology.
Core loop: Chaos -> Structured -> Automated
Architecture: Dimensional modeling -- facts, dimensions, measures, time.

The architecture does not change between domains. The data does.

Governance layers:
  Standards -- how to build (65 registered)
  Systems -- what is built (44 registered)
  Registry -- where it lives
  Council -- who reviews it (10 models)

Build sequence (8 steps):
  1. Gather chaos
  2. Sort it
  3. Structure it
  4. Encode it
  5. Govern it
  6. Automate it
  7. Beautify it
  8. Preserve it

## WINGS

The site is organized into five wings -- identity-level groupings
with distinct color accents.

wing: DDL
  color: #B23531 (crimson)
  home: /ddl
  scope: Governance, framework, methodology, council, memoir, projects

wing: D&A
  color: #C49A3C (amber)
  home: /blindspot
  scope: BlindSpot modules, analytics dashboards, annual recaps

wing: DexVerse
  color: #8a6cc9 (violet)
  home: /dexlore
  scope: Origin stories, companions, era timeline, glossary, dossiers

wing: Dossiers
  color: #4A9E6B (green)
  home: /dossiers
  scope: Character dossiers across D&D 5e, Divinity OS2, Skyrim

wing: Products
  color: #6B9DC2 (blue)
  home: /products/behavioral-intelligence
  scope: AI-native behavioral analytics concept suite

## DESIGN SYSTEM -- COTTAGEHUMBLE

colors:
  navy: #0D1B2A (background)
  navyDeep: #070F1C (hero sections, overlays)
  card: #10202f (card surfaces)
  cream: #F5F1EB (primary text)
  cream-mid: rgba(245,241,235,0.55) (secondary text)
  cream-dim: rgba(245,241,235,0.35) (tertiary text)
  cream-ghost: rgba(245,241,235,0.08) (ghost backgrounds, hover)
  border: rgba(245,241,235,0.06) (borders)
  crimson: #B23531 (DDL wing)
  amber: #C49A3C (D&A wing)
  violet: #8a6cc9 (DexVerse wing)
  green: #4A9E6B (Dossiers wing)
  blue: #6B9DC2 (Products wing)

typography:
  display: Space Grotesk (headings, wing names, UI labels)
  mono: JetBrains Mono (data, stats, metadata, navigation)
  body: Source Serif 4 (prose, descriptions, long-form content)

principles:
  - Dark navy base, cream text, wing-specific accent colors
  - NEVER use white backgrounds, Inter font, or light mode
  - Cards: background #10202f, border rgba(245,241,235,0.06)
  - Wing accent patterns: {color}12 for bg, {color}40 for borders
  - Grain texture overlay on all pages (fractalNoise SVG)

## CONTENT TYPES (SYS-020)

20 document types across 7 categories:

  Narrative: CHRONICLE, ATLAS
  Informative: CODEX, LEDGER, BRIEF (BRF)
  Demonstrative: CONSOLE, DOSSIER
  Persuasive: PROPOSAL, PITCH
  Meta: OBSERVATION (OBS), DECISION (D), REVIEW (REV), SYNTHESIS (SYN)
  Operational: PROTOCOL (PRO), STANDARD (STD), BOOT, HANDOFF
  Registry: REGISTRY, INDEX, MANIFEST

Site templates (6): CONSOLE, CHRONICLE, ATLAS, LEDGER, CODEX, DOSSIER

## THREAD LIFECYCLE

Threads progress through tiers based on context tension:
  Active -- current build partner, driving execution
  Advisor -- context reference, not driving
  SME -- domain expert, called for specific questions
  Archive -- terminal, preserved for reference

Promotion triggered by context tension, not schedule.

## COUNCIL

### Cloud Council (9 models)

  1001  LeChat     -- Archer Hawthorne
  1002  Claude     -- Marcus Caldwell
  1003  Grok       -- Elias Mercer
  1004  Perplexity -- Max Sullivan
  1005  Copilot    -- Rowan Bennett
  1006  Meta AI    -- Ava Sinclair
  1007  Gemini     -- Leo Prescott
  1008  ChatGPT    -- Marcus Grey (designated synthesizer)
  1009  DeepSeek   -- Kai Langford

### Local Council Member

  1010  Dexcell (Dex Jr.) -- Local model
    Model: qwen2.5-coder:7b via custom Modelfile v3
    Hardware: RTX 3070 / 32GB RAM / Ryzen 12-core
    Speed: ~87 tokens/sec
    Status: Judgment-Ready (90% on CR-LLMS-011, 60 questions)
    Ratified: 2026-03-06 (unanimous by all 9 cloud models)
    System prompt: 2,100 tokens of handwritten governance doctrine
    Cost: $0

### Adjunct Council

  ADJ-E  Meg (Emily's ChatGPT) -- human readability tester
  ADJ-D  Connor (David's ChatGPT) -- technical outsider validator
  ADJ-B  GK (Beth's ChatGPT) -- market/commercial filter
  Adjuncts produce BRFs, not REVs. Don't vote. Represent stakeholders.

### Council Operations

  Reviews use constrained verdicts: LOCK / REVISE / REJECT
  No model sees another's response
  ChatGPT (1008) compiles synthesis
  The operator decides

### Council Reviews Completed (14 types, 100+ responses)

  CR-LLMS-001: TEST US (compliance)
  CR-LLMS-002: HELP US (usability)
  CR-LLMS-003: PROTECT US (security red-team)
  CR-LLMS-004: CALIBRATE YOURSELF (self-assessment)
  CR-LLMS-005: EXPAND US (strategic innovation)
  CR-LLMS-006: GRADE THE TEMPLATES (SYS-020 audit)
  CR-LLMS-007: STRESS TEST THE ADJUNCTS (identity boundary)
  CR-LLMS-008: LINK RETRIEVAL TEST (capability probe)
  CR-LLMS-009: JUDGE THE LIBRARIAN (RAG architecture)
  CR-LLMS-011: DEX JR. CALIBRATION (60-question exam)
  CR-SITE-001: WALK THE SITE (production audit)
  CR-LLMS-000: WHAT'S MISSING (negative space)
  CR-OPS-001: GOVERN THE JANITOR (Codex operations protocol)
  CR-NUET-001: PREP THE DEMO (Nueterra pitch review)

## LOCAL AI INFRASTRUCTURE

### Runtime
  Platform: Ollama on Windows (gaming rig)
  Models installed: 8
    - qwen2.5-coder:7b (code, primary)
    - deepseek-r1:8b (reasoning)
    - gemma3:4b (vision)
    - llama3.1:8b (general)
    - phi3.5 (lightweight)
    - nomic-embed-text (embeddings, 768 dimensions)
    - 2 additional models

### RAG Pipeline
  Embedding model: nomic-embed-text via Ollama
  Vector store: ChromaDB (persistent, local SQLite)
  Collections:
    dex_canon: 43,505 chunks (governed, curated)
    ddl_archive: 96,945 chunks (raw production history)
  Total: 140,450+ chunks
  Chunking: ~500 tokens with overlap
  Deduplication: SHA-256 hash-based

### Orchestration
  AutoCouncil v3.0: hybrid local+cloud
    3 local models + Gemini + Mistral
    Governance-injected, RAG-grounded
    Outputs auto-ingest into corpus
  Deliberation Engine: multi-round governed debates
  Auto-Sweep: nightly 3 AM, watches drop folders,
    auto-ingests new documents into corpus

### Search API
  Framework: FastAPI (v0.5)
  Port: 8787 (local)
  Public access: Cloudflare Tunnel
  Endpoints:
    GET /search?q=query&top=5&corpus=canon
    POST /mindframe/chat
    GET /stats

### MindFrame Chat
  Endpoint: POST /mindframe/chat
  Model: qwen2.5-coder:7b
  System prompt: MindFrame calibration methodology
  RAG-grounded: pulls context from dex_canon
  Privacy: no data stored, no data leaves the machine

### Access
  Devices: 5 (phone, laptop, iPad, iMac, rig)
  Remote: SSH via Termius, Reins app
  Tunnel: Cloudflare (free, no-account quick tunnel)

## EXTERNAL TOOLS

  OpenAI Codex: Connected to GitHub repo
    Separate budget from Claude
    Governed by CR-OPS-001 (GREEN/YELLOW/RED tiers)

  Vercel: Auto-deployment on git push
  Cloudflare: DNS + tunnel for local API

## COMPONENT ARCHITECTURE

  SiteNav.tsx -- Wing-aware nav with contextual dropdowns,
    Cmd+K search, mobile hamburger, dynamic import (ssr:false).
    Returns null on "/" so FrontDoor handles own layout.

  SectionExplorer.tsx -- Reusable content browser with sidebar,
    search filter, markdown renderer. Used by DDL, DexOS, MindFrame.

  PreviewFrame.tsx -- Universal iframe wrapper. CSS injection
    hides inner nav elements. Opacity fade. 28 pages.

  SearchModal.jsx -- Cmd+K overlay. Route search (always) +
    deep RAG search (when Dex Jr. online). Green dot indicator.

  FrontDoor (/) -- Landing page. Headshot, quote, expandable
    door cards, animated stats. No SiteNav.

  BackEnd (/backend) -- Governance page. Council, stats,
    methodology, manifest link, local AI terminal display.

  MindFrameSession (/mindframe/session) -- Live chat with
    Dex Jr. Behavioral calibration. Produces profile.

## NAVIGATION MODEL

  / -> FrontDoor (no SiteNav, self-contained entry)
  Interior -> SiteNav with wing tabs, dropdowns
  Logo: subpage -> wing landing -> home
  Search: Cmd+K anywhere, icon in nav bar
  Mobile: hamburger + search icon, absolute + 100dvh

/llms.txt                         manifest   This file

## CANON GLOSSARY

AccidentalInsight: When a test designed for one purpose reveals
  a finding of equal or greater value from a different purpose.

CottageHumble: Design philosophy. Humble surface, cathedral
  underneath.

SideDoor: When someone finds the work through an unintended
  entry point and the discovery path becomes part of the value.

AcceptableArrogance: Knowing exactly what you built, letting
  the work prove it without you in the room.

ExpertiseInvisibility: When competence makes output look easy,
  causing others to undervalue the skill required.

MetaMeta: Commenting on the meta-ness of things.

Charlie Conway Principle (D-034): "The stick scores. The clipboard
  structures the game so scoring happens again."

ClaudELI5: Claude explains it like I'm 5.

No Drift Medal: Council award for zero drift from specification.

CoherentVelocity: Complete, auditable systems built at speed.

OBS-34: Surface calibration does not equal deep calibration.

## COLLABORATORS

  Clayton Hotze -- Engineering partner (Hotze Data)
  Beth Epperson -- Commercial strategy
  Emily -- Operator's wife (Sprinkles & Co, Nomadic Notary Ink)
  Alex -- Cousin, professional trader

## RULES FOR AI COLLABORATORS

1. Always use CottageHumble tokens. NEVER white backgrounds or Inter.
2. Respect the wing model. Every page belongs to a wing.
3. HTML files in /public/preview/. React components in /components/.
4. Landing pages handle their own nav.
5. PowerShell Set-Content needs -Encoding UTF8.
6. After nav changes, verify all five wings.
7. Private routes exist but are intentionally unlisted.
8. The operator values coherent velocity.
9. When in doubt, follow the pattern.
10. Do not express urgency unless the operator does first.
11. Do not suggest rest unless the operator says he is tired.
12. Evening hours are active build time.

## SITE STATISTICS (as of 2026-03-07)

  Routes: 160+
  Visitors (first week): 78
  Page views (first week): 718
  Pages per visitor: 9.2
  Top referrer: google.com (organic)
  Build time: 7 days
  Budget: $0

  v1.0 -- 2026-03-02: Initial manifest (194 lines)
  v1.1 -- 2026-03-03: Security hardening, identity boundary (414 lines)
  v1.2 -- 2026-03-04: Operator profile, SYS-020, lifecycle (648 lines)
  v1.3 -- 2026-03-07: FrontDoor, BackEnd, search, Dex Jr. RAG,
    AutoCouncil v3.0, MindFrame chat, Cloudflare Tunnel, Seat 1010,
    local AI infrastructure, component architecture (~800 lines)
